title: match — published events
description: Every event implements `ShouldDispatchAfterCommit` so listeners never fire against a rolled-back write. MatchLineupPublished + MatchResultPublished are the load-bearing signals — the first triggers family + public notify + attendance auto-materialisation; the second triggers competition-standings recompute + parent notifications + awards/MOTM streak evaluation. MatchPostponed + MatchCancelled + MatchAbandoned close the fixture and re-notify parents.

events.name: MatchScheduled
events.trigger: MatchObserver.creating committed a new matches row. Attaches to a Competition when competition_id IS NOT NULL.
consumers: match::CacheOpponentLogoOnMatchScheduled (writes / bumps the opponent_logos row for the named opponent)

events.name: MatchLineupSet
events.trigger: Match.status transitions SCHEDULED -> LINEUP_SET. Head coach chose the squad + roles. Lineup is not yet parent-facing — that requires MatchLineupPublished.

events.name: MatchLineupPublished
events.trigger: POST /matches/{match}/lineup/publish committed. lineup_published_at set. Kicks parent-facing notify + attendance auto-materialisation.
consumers: match::MaterialiseAttendanceFromLineupJob (creates pre-filled attendance rows from the published squad)

events.name: MatchStarted
events.trigger: Match.status transitions LINEUP_SET -> IN_PROGRESS. Typically automatic at kickoff_at or manual from a coach starting the timer.
consumers: match::MaterialiseAttendanceOnMatchStarted (freezes pre-filled attendance rows into the confirmable set)

events.name: MatchScored
events.trigger: A match_events row with type IN ('goal', 'own_goal', 'apparatus_score', 'lane_time') was inserted while status=IN_PROGRESS. Powers the live scoreboard.

events.name: MatchCompleted
events.trigger: Match.status transitions IN_PROGRESS -> COMPLETED. Requires at least one match_results row present.

events.name: MatchPostponed
events.trigger: POST /matches/{match}/postpone. Match keeps its identity + squad + notes but kickoff_at is re-planned. Not terminal.

payload.new_kickoff_at: ISO 8601 | null (null when re-scheduling is deferred)

events.name: MatchCancelled
events.trigger: POST /matches/{match}/cancel. Terminal. Match is preserved for audit; no future rescheduling.

events.name: MatchAbandoned
events.trigger: POST /matches/{match}/abandon. Only from status=IN_PROGRESS. Terminal. Partial match_events are preserved but no result publish.
consumers: audit::WriteToAuditLog (retention: 7 years — abandonments are compliance-relevant for injury / safeguarding claims)

events.name: MatchResultPublished
events.trigger: POST /matches/{match}/results/publish. match_results.published_at set on all owned rows. Terminal for scoring. Kicks the standings recompute.
consumers: match::RecomputeStandingsOnMatchResultPublished (dispatches RecomputeStandingsJob when competition_id IS NOT NULL)

events.name: MatchMotmAwarded
events.trigger: match_results.motm_athlete_id was set (or changed) while published_at IS NULL. Distinct from MatchResultPublished so the awards module can react without waiting for the full publish.
